metric_value: Billion-dollar-plus market
Growing in excess of 150% per year
Systems approach: full-range solutions across all four market segments (enterprise, SOHO, individual, ISP/carrier) with focus on total cost of ownership reduction
30-35% of cost of ownership is line costs; 40-45% is support requirements
notes: 3Com's TCO focus justified by this data

metric_name: Remote access market segment 1: Enterprise customer
metric_value: Data-centric mainframe roots; needs lower TCO, legacy support, security, investment protection; 3Com targets with AccessBuilder high-performance servers

metric_name: Remote access market segment 2: SOHO (Small Office/Home Office)
metric_value: Fastest growing segment; Aberdeen considers 3Com best-established and most competitive here

metric_name: Remote access market segment 3: Individual remote user
metric_value: Foundation of remote access market; 3Com targets with Impact ISDN modems and AccessBuilder Internet

metric_name: Remote access market segment 4: Internet/Online Service Providers
metric_value: New segment requiring robustness, reliability, scalability; 3Com targeting with AccessBuilder 5000 and 8000
Centrum Communications acquired February 1994; brought AccessBuilder product line
Acquired June 1995; dominant provider of integrated remote access to network service providers worldwide
Free ISDN line ordering via 800-number team; 3Com provisions ISDN for homes, small offices and branch offices
7 and 17-slot chassis; up to 256 WAN ports; multiple LAN segments; RISC processors; Ethernet or Token-Ring
Coherent framework for scaling performance, growth management, and network reach from core to remote; 3Com's consistent management philosophy
Aberdeen expects 3Com to continue enjoying increasing success in remote access, especially SOHO; enterprise/ISP segments will be harder to crack
3Com gained dominance in SOHO/consumer modems via USRobotics merger; failed to establish enterprise/ISP leadership; exited enterprise networking entirely in 2000; acquired by HP 2010 for $2.7B
notes: Partially correct: SOHO success confirmed; enterprise challenge proved fatal to that segment strategy

metric_name: 3Com channel strategy as greatest competitive asset
metric_value: 3Com's channels are its most valued asset and most important element in its remote access strategy
notes: Aberdeen identifies channels as key differentiator

metric_value: Channel strength proved durable for SOHO segment but insufficient for enterprise/carrier; 3Com's Enterprise segment discontinuation in 2000 confirmed Aberdeen's enterprise-challenge prediction
notes: Verified via 3Com Wikipedia and HP acquisition press release

metric_name: ISDN role in remote access growth
metric_value: ISDN availability becoming more widespread and tariffing more reasonable; key technology for 3Com's SOHO growth
notes: ISDN later displaced by DSL and cable broadband

metric_value: Two global business units: one for low/mid-tier customers, one for high-end enterprise/ISP/telco; positions company to address full remote access spectrum
Range from 15-minute install terminal adapters to complex integrated access switching platforms with ISP/carrier-grade redundancy
